message:
fix(packs/monitoring/prometheus): close quote-bypass on prometheus-rules-file-delete

Extends the shell-quote-bypass sweep to the prometheus monitoring
pack. `rm /etc/prometheus/rules.yaml` was caught; `rm "/etc/
prometheus/rules.yaml"` and `rm '/etc/prometheus/rules.d/myrule.
yaml'` were not, for the same shell-unquoting reason as the rm-rf
fix: bash unquotes before rm sees its argv.

Accept `['"]?` immediately before the absolute path in the
destructive pattern:

  \brm\b(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*\s+(?:(?:-f|--force)\s+)?['"]?
  (?:/etc/prometheus/(?:rules\.d|rules)/\S+|/etc/prometheus/
  (?:prometheus|rules)\.(?:ya?ml))['"]?(?:\s|$)

Unquoted form still matches. Quoted / half-quoted forms now also
match. No pattern weakened.

files.patch:
@@ -65,7 +65,7 @@ fn create_destructive_patterns() -> Vec<DestructivePattern> {
     vec![
         destructive_pattern!(
             "prometheus-rules-file-delete",
-            r"\brm\b(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*\s+(?:(?:-f|--force)\s+)?(?:/etc/prometheus/(?:rules\.d|rules)/\S+|/etc/prometheus/(?:prometheus|rules)\.(?:ya?ml))(?:\s|$)",
+            r#"\brm\b(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*\s+(?:(?:-f|--force)\s+)?['"]?(?:/etc/prometheus/(?:rules\.d|rules)/\S+|/etc/prometheus/(?:prometheus|rules)\.(?:ya?ml))['"]?(?:\s|$)"#,
             "Deleting Prometheus rule/config files can break alerting and monitoring coverage.",
             Critical,
             "Deleting Prometheus configuration or rule files stops alerting for the defined \
